title: Bitter Gooey Middle Eastern Dates Stir-fry
description: A festive and indulgent dessert dish featuring dark rum-soaked dates in a rich, aromatic bhuna sauce, perfect for an evening treat.

ingredients.name: 10 ounces dark dates
ingredients.notes: Pitted and soaked in rum for 4 hours.

instructions: Rinse pearl barley under cold water and soak in warm water for about 10 minutes. Drain the water from the dates.

In a saucepan, combine soaked dates with pearl barley, water, honey or stevia sweetener, and lemon juice. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer until the liquid is absorbed by the grains (about 25-30 minutes).
Heat ghee in a wok or skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and grated ginger; sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add bhuna masala powder to the wok, cooking it gently without browning to prevent burning. Stir in the cooked barley-date mixture and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Fold in soaked dates, adjusting sweetness if necessary based on your preference. Serve hot garnished with fresh mint leaves if desired.
